summary.text: Historical data is provided by S&P Global Market Intelligence. Real-time stock price data is provided by CBOE and 15-minute delayed consolidated tape pricing data is provided by Nasdaq UTP. Several fund data points are also provided by Finnhub.

overview.description: The American Conservative Values ETF (ACVF) is an exchange-traded fund that mostly invests in large cap equity. The fund is an actively managed fund of large-cap US companies that are perceived to align with political conservative values. ACVF was launched on Oct 29, 2020 and is issued by ACV.

data.title: This hidden investing flaw is costing you money. Talking to political opponents fixes it.
data.text: The stock market doesn't care about your politics. Insisting on a portfolio that's red or blue can forfeit a lot of green.

data.title: Is Nvidia a Republican or a Democrat? These political ETFs have voted.
data.text: ETFs that combine investing and politics tap into investors' polarized views. Why they shouldn't get your vote.

holdings.infoBox: ACVF is an equity ETF with a total of 391 individual holdings. The top holdings are NVIDIA stock at 8.20%, Microsoft at 4.17%, Cisco Systems at 2.96%, Broadcom at 2.72%, and Walmart at 1.77%.
